---
title: "Blog Post Template"
description: "A starter template showing the Quarto features most useful for data analytics and ML blog posts."
author: "Jonathan Pearce"
date: "2026-01-01"
image: thumbnail.png
draft: true
toc: true
code-fold: true
code-copy: true
number-sections: true
fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

<!-- 
  HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE
  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1. Copy this folder (blog/posts/template-post/) and rename it to your post slug.
  2. Rename or replace thumbnail.png with your own image.
  3. Update the YAML front-matter above (title, description, date, categories, etc.).
  4. Set draft: false when you are ready to publish.
  5. Write your post below using the features demonstrated here.
-->

## Introduction

Start with a brief overview of the problem or topic.
Use **bold** and *italic* for emphasis, and [links](https://quarto.org) where needed.

::: {.callout-note}
## Key Takeaway
Callout boxes are great for highlighting important points.
Other types: `.callout-warning`, `.callout-tip`, `.callout-important`.
:::

## Data & Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Import libraries"

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(42)
```

## Analysis

### Generating Sample Data

```{python}
#| label: fig-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plot of two correlated variables."

x = np.random.normal(0, 1, 200)
y = 0.8 * x + np.random.normal(0, 0.5, 200)

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
plt.scatter(x, y, alpha=0.6, edgecolors="k", linewidth=0.5)
plt.xlabel("Feature X")
plt.ylabel("Target Y")
plt.title("Sample Scatter Plot")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: tbl-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Summary statistics for the sample data."

df = pd.DataFrame({"x": x, "y": y})
df.describe().round(2)
```

### Tabsets for Comparisons

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Model A

```{python}
from numpy.polynomial import polynomial as P

coeffs = np.polyfit(x, y, 1)
print(f"Linear fit: y = {coeffs[0]:.3f}x + {coeffs[1]:.3f}")
```

#### Model B

```{python}
coeffs2 = np.polyfit(x, y, 2)
print(f"Quadratic fit: y = {coeffs2[0]:.3f}x² + {coeffs2[1]:.3f}x + {coeffs2[2]:.3f}")
```

:::

## Mathematical Notation

Inline math: $\hat{y} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x$

Display math:

$$
\mathcal{L}(\theta) = -\frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}\left[y_i \log \hat{y}_i + (1 - y_i)\log(1 - \hat{y}_i)\right]
$$

## Diagrams with Mermaid

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Raw Data] --> B[Clean & Transform]
    B --> C[Feature Engineering]
    C --> D[Model Training]
    D --> E[Evaluation]
    E --> F{Good enough?}
    F -- Yes --> G[Deploy]
    F -- No --> C
```

## Conclusion

Summarise findings and next steps here.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
